title: TMDB Movie Keyword SCL Scores
subtitle: 246K movies with SCL-filtered keyword valence aggregates
description:
One row per TMDB movie. Keyword valence is aggregated using the SCL (Sentiment Composition Lexicon) hierarchy — OPP > NMA > VAD — and filtered to exclude metadata keywords (place names, time periods, genre labels, source material, structural trivia, adult content) that carry word-level connotation rather than narrative emotional content.

## Pipeline

```
TMDB movies (bdelanghe/tmdb-movies-clean)
  → explode keywords (1 row per movie×keyword)
  → join keyword lexicon (bdelanghe/tmdb-keyword-lexicon)
  → filter exclude_from_scl = False
  → aggregate per movie
```

## Columns

| column | description |
|--------|-------------|
| `id` | TMDB movie ID |
| `title` | Movie title |
| `release_date` | Release date |
| `vote_average` | TMDB user rating |
| `vote_count` | Number of votes |
| `popularity` | TMDB popularity score |
| `genres` | Comma-separated genre list |
| `total_keywords` | All keywords attached to this movie |
| `excluded_keyword_count` | Keywords excluded from SCL aggregation (metadata/structural) |
| `matched_keyword_count` | SCL-eligible keywords with a valence score |
| `keyword_match_rate` | matched / (total − excluded) |
| `avg_keyword_valence` | Mean valence across SCL-eligible matched keywords (bipolar −1 to +1) |
| `min_keyword_valence` | Most negative single keyword |
| `max_keyword_valence` | Most positive single keyword |
| `valence_std` | Spread of keyword sentiment — high = tonally mixed |
| `in_scl_count` | Keywords with exact SCL phrase scores |
| `has_nma_count` | Keywords containing a negator, modal, or adverb |
| `shift_strong_count` | Keywords where phrase valence diverges strongly from token average |
| `avg_composition_shift` | Mean compositional surprise across in-SCL keywords |
| `max_abs_composition_shift` | Strongest single compositional shift in the keyword set |
| `composition_type_dominant` | Most common linguistic mechanism (direct, opposing_polarity, idiomatic, …) |

## Keyword type filter

See `bdelanghe/tmdb-keyword-lexicon` for the full keyword taxonomy. The `exclude_from_scl` flag excludes: `structural`, `source_material`, `adult`, `time_period`, `setting_place`, `film_form`, `identity_social`, `genre_label`. Retained for valence: `character`, `theme`, `other`.

## Source

Built by [bdelanghe/imdb-kaggle](https://github.com/bdelanghe/imdb-kaggle). SCL lexicons by Saif M. Mohammad (NRC Canada).
